commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add mistletoe-based Python build (#137)

* Initial plan

* Add build.py: dependency-free Python Jekyll replacement

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: remove __pycache__ from repo, improve where_exp filter, cleanup

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace regex preprocessing with custom Liquid tags for include and seo

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add uv metadata, pygments highlighting, toc/no_toc, smarty-pants, fix rss.xsl

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Improve TOC: match by heading text instead of re-computed IDs, use DOTALL

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix smarty-pants corrupting HTML attributes; use custom dash conversion instead

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor build.py: proper pipeline layers, ET-based TOC, HTML-layer smart dashes

- Move smart-dash conversion from markdown pre-processing to HTML
  post-processing (operates on rendered text nodes, not raw source)
- Use markdown2's `toc` extra + ElementTree for TOC filtering instead
  of regex-parsing HTML to build/filter TOC
- Pre-process {% link %} tags once at load time instead of on every
  render call with loader swapping
- Simplify render_liquid (no more site_cfg param, no loader swapping)
- Normalize kramdown \ linebreaks to standard markdown (two trailing
  spaces) before rendering
- Explicitly import pygments dependency

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Swap markdown2 for mistune: fixes [[link]] bug, native \ breaks, Pygments highlighting

- Replace markdown2 with mistune for Markdown rendering
- Fixes markdown2 bug where multiple [[text](url)] on same line
  only converted the last one (PDF/DOI/ARX links now all work)
- Backslash line breaks (\\ at EOL) handled natively by mistune
- Custom _JekyllRenderer: Pygments code highlighting, heading IDs,
  TOC collection during rendering (no HTML re-parsing needed)
- TOC filtering by excluded title is a simple list comprehension
  in render_toc() instead of ElementTree/regex post-processing
- Raw HTML passthrough (escape=False) for inline HTML like
  the Mastodon link with display:none

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix blog page: collapse blank lines inside HTML blocks before mistune rendering

Liquid templates produce blank lines from {% for %}/{% unless %} tags.
Combined with indentation inside <ul>/<div>/etc, mistune misparsed the
content as indented code blocks.  _collapse_html_blanks() removes blank
lines while inside block-level HTML elements before passing to mistune.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: reuse HtmlFormatter, optimize _collapse_html_blanks regex

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix highlight CSS class and footnotes selector to match mistune output

- Change Pygments cssclass from 'codehilite' to 'highlight' to match
  the existing CSS rules in main.scss
- Update footnotes CSS selectors from 'div.footnotes' to also match
  'section.footnotes' (mistune generates <section> not <div>)

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix smart-dashes breaking HTML comments by skipping <!-- ... --> blocks

The _smart_dashes_html regex was converting -- inside HTML comments
to en-dashes, turning --> into –> and breaking comment closing tags.
This caused everything after the broken comment to be swallowed
(hiding footnotes and footer on 11 pages including compiler-effects).

Add <!--.*?--> to the _HTML_SKIP_RE pattern so comments are preserved.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add golden test: checksums for all rendered HTML/XML/CSS files

test_golden.py rebuilds the site and compares SHA256 checksums against
golden_checksums.json.  Run with --update to refresh after intentional
changes; run without flags to verify the build output is unchanged.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace smart-dash post-processing with InlineParser subclass

Use _SmartDashInlineParser to handle --- and -- conversion during
parsing instead of regex post-processing on rendered HTML.  This
correctly preserves dashes inside code spans (e.g. `--zjit`, `--flag`)
which were previously mangled to en-dashes.

Raw HTML blocks still get smart-dash conversion via block_html() in
the renderer, since they bypass inline parsing.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment documenting regex lookahead in SmartDashInlineParser

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix two bugs found by Jekyll comparison: span markdown rendering and timezone dates

1. Markdown inside <span data-nosnippet> was not rendered (12 posts affected).
   Fix: preprocess multi-line inline HTML elements to single lines so mistune's
   inline parser handles the markdown content.

2. Dates with timezone abbreviations (PST/PDT/EDT) were off by one day (7 posts).
   Fix: convert to UTC using known timezone offsets before formatting, matching
   Jekyll's behavior.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rename _INLINE_HTML_BLOCK_RE to _INLINE_SPAN_RE and clarify comment

Address code review: the regex only handles <span> elements, so the name
and comment should be specific rather than implying other inline elements.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add language-plaintext highlighter-rouge class to inline code, CI workflow, and fly.io build.py deploy

- Add codespan() to _JekyllRenderer for inline code class matching Jekyll
- Add .github/workflows/build-py.yml CI (build + golden test)
- Update Dockerfile to use build.py for fly.io preview deploys
- Update golden checksums for inline code class change

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove golden test from build.py CI workflow; keep only build step

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Wrap bare code blocks in language-plaintext highlighter-rouge div for consistent yellow background

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix mistune footnote bug: fenced code blocks truncated inside footnotes

Monkey-patch REF_FOOTNOTE regex to allow >4 spaces indentation in
continuation lines. Add minimal repro script for upstream report.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add build_mistletoe.py: mistletoe-based static site builder

Replace all mistune usage in build.py with mistletoe equivalents:
- Custom HtmlRenderer with Pygments highlighting and heading IDs
- Footnote pre-processing (parse definitions/references before rendering)
- Smart dashes as post-processing (skip <pre>, <code>, comments, tags)
- Output to mistletoe-build/ directory for diffing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add build_mistletoe.py: experimental mistletoe-based markdown renderer

Initial working version that builds all 367 pages. Key differences
from mistune version: footnotes via preprocessing, smart dashes via
post-processing. 128 of 367 output files differ - iterating toward parity.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rewrite mistletoe markdown pipeline with AST-level footnotes and smart dashes

Replace regex-based footnote preprocessing and smart-dash post-processing
with proper mistletoe custom tokens:
- FootnoteDef: block token that parses [^key]: definitions
- FootnoteRef: span token that matches [^key] references
- SmartDash: span token for --- and -- conversion

Footnote bodies are parsed as sub-documents with parent link-reference
definitions shared, so [text][ref] links resolve correctly.

Remove checked-in build directories from git tracking.
Match Jekyll output format (div.footnotes, reversefootnote class, key-based IDs).

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Replace build.py with mistletoe-based renderer, remove mistune

- Rename build_mistletoe.py → build.py (output to _site instead of mistletoe-build/)
- Remove old mistune-based build.py
- Update CI workflow: pip install mistletoe instead of mistune
- Update Dockerfile: pip install mistletoe instead of mistune
- Update golden checksums for new renderer output
- Clean up stale build_mistletoe.py reference in page exclude list

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update Dockerfile to use uv instead of pip for faster builds

Use ghcr.io/astral-sh/uv:python3.12-alpine base image and `uv run build.py`
which reads PEP 723 inline script metadata directly from build.py.
No separate pip install step needed.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix stray dash bullets in favorites.md to use consistent asterisk markers

Lines 27-28 used `-` while the rest of the list used `*`, causing
mistletoe to render three separate <ul> lists instead of one.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Clean up PR: remove golden tests/repros, simplify CSS footnotes, use uv in CI

- Remove golden_checksums.json, test_golden.py, mistune_footnote_bug_repro.py
- Remove section.footnotes CSS selectors (footnotes render as div now)
- Switch CI workflow from pip+setup-python to uv via astral-sh/setup-uv
- Clean .gitignore to only add __pycache__/ vs main

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix analytics: read JEKYLL_ENV so Google Analytics and GoatCounter render in production

build.py was hardcoding jekyll.environment to "development", so the
analytics Liquid guard never passed. Now reads JEKYLL_ENV env var
(default: "development"). Dockerfile sets JEKYLL_ENV=production for
the fly.io deploy build stage.

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Revert Dockerfile to development mode so PR previews don't count as production traffic

Co-authored-by: tekknolagi <401167+tekknolagi@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove never-hit e2e caches (~2.5 min/run saved) (#390)

* Initial plan

* Remove unused e2e caches that are never hit (~2.5 min/run saved)

Investigation of recent CI runs confirmed all three caches (Docker
images, node_modules, Playwright browsers) always showed "Cache not
found" — because e2e only runs on PRs (never main), so branch-scoped
GitHub Actions caches are never populated for PR branches to inherit.

Removes:
- 3 actions/cache steps (Docker images, node_modules, Playwright)
- Explicit "Save Docker images for caching" step
- Conditional if guards on Docker Buildx/login/build steps
- Cache-hit conditional logic in the install step

Keeps:
- Docker BuildKit layer caching (type=gha) which works cross-branch

Co-authored-by: jeffredodd <2861516+jeffredodd@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: switch release profile from opt-level=s to opt-level=2

opt-level=s (size-optimize) traded ~10-42% performance for ~1MB smaller
binary. For a desktop application, prefer speed over binary size:

Benchmark improvements (opt-level=s → opt-level=2):
- height_est_100kb: 105µs → 90µs (15% faster)
- parse_100kb_unicode: 995µs → 805µs (19% faster)
- full_pipeline_100kb: 3.63ms → 3.01ms (17% faster)
- viewport_scan_200kb: 929ns → 537ns (42% faster)
- cache_flip_50kb: 2.11ms → 1.87ms (11% faster)

Binary size: 5.7MB → 6.7MB (still compact with LTO + strip)

fix(clippy): suppress aarch64 NEON clippy warnings in weight dequant (#3300)

Added file-level clippy allow attributes to neon_weight_dequant.rs and
neon_weight_dequant_v2.rs for manual_is_multiple_of,
needless_range_loop, and related lints.

macOS ARM64 Clippy job fails with 129 errors on every PR. These files
are aarch64-gated so errors only appear on ARM runners. The pattern is
already established in other NEON kernel files.

## What was tested
- cargo clippy on x86 passes cleanly
- CI will validate on macOS ARM64

None - style lints in unsafe NEON intrinsic code.

commit.message:
test: add 67 game telemetry integration tests

Add 67 integration tests covering game-specific telemetry parsing,
adapter registration/discovery, packet routing, config generation,
game auto-detection, multi-game concurrency, rate limiting, data
invariants, cross-game normalization consistency, and error handling.

Games tested include Forza Motorsport (UDP binary), Live for Speed
(OutGauge UDP), Rennsport (binary UDP), WRC Generations (Codemasters
Mode 1 UDP), SimHub (JSON UDP), and MudRunner (JSON UDP).
